note: Replay plan events were derived from effective adapter replay_events generated from source test evidence.

events.description: CharReaderAllowSpecialFloatsTest.specialFloat: parse NaN and Infinity without allowSpecialFloats should fail.
events.evidence: src/test_lib_json/main.cpp line 3736: JSONTEST_ASSERT(!ok) for NaN and Infinity.
events.input: JSON strings with NaN or Infinity values

expected.observable_behavior: parse returns error (Err) for both inputs

events.description: CharReaderFailIfExtraTest.commentAfterBool: parse bool with trailing comment and failIfExtra=true should succeed and root asBool equals true.

event_metadata.normalization: parse returns Ok and as_bool returns Some(true)

events.input: " true /*trailing\ncomment*/"

expected.observable_behavior: parse succeeds and parsed value is true

events.description: Parse booleans with comments and line endings; first succeeds, others fail due to extra after comment.

event_metadata.normalization: parse result (Ok/Err) and root.as_bool() when Ok

events.input: parse three documents: " true //comment1...", " true //com\rment", " true //com\nment"

expected.observable_behavior: First document parses successfully and root.as_bool() is Some(true); second and third documents fail.

events.description: Remove member with key "top" from object containing binary string value (with null byte) and verify removed value matches, then remove same key again and verify returned value is Null.

event_metadata.normalization: Equality of returned JsonValue with expected JsonValue::String using PartialEq.

input.case: Object with single key 'top' mapped to JsonValue::String containing null byte ('h\0i\0')

expected.observable_behavior: First remove returns JsonValue::String equal to binary; second remove returns JsonValue::Null.

events.description: Remove member with binary string key containing null byte from object and verify removed value matches, then remove same key again and verify returned value is Null.
events.evidence: src/test_lib_json/main.cpp ValueTest.zeroesInKeys: removeMember with binary key returns "there"; second remove returns falsy and removed unchanged.

event_metadata.normalization: Equality of returned JsonValue with expected JsonValue::String using PartialEq.

input.case: Object with single key (binary string containing null byte 'h\0i\0') mapped to JsonValue::String "there"

expected.observable_behavior: First remove returns JsonValue::String "there"; second remove returns JsonValue::Null.
